summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Patient reports an inability to donate blood due to anemia and fatigue.

History of Present Illness
The patient reports a long-standing history of iron deficiency anemia, dating back to age 13. She reports feeling fatigued frequently throughout the day and experiencing occasional feverish chills. She also notes episodes of wheezing and headaches, which are not typical for her. She reports heavy menstrual periods prior to her hysterectomy in 1996. She has not received a recent colonoscopy and reports it was performed approximately five to six years ago with no polyps noted. She currently takes ferrous sulfate 25 mg twice daily and vitamin B12 over-the-counter. She denies any history of sedation issues.

Findings
Physical exam reveals normal findings without significant findings. Vital signs are within normal limits. The patient denies any pacemaker or defibrillator use, sleep apnea, or oxygen use at night.

Assessment
The patient presents with iron deficiency anemia and associated symptoms of fatigue, chills, and headaches. Further investigation is warranted to determine the etiology of the anemia.

Plan
Order an upper endoscopy and colonoscopy to evaluate for sources of bleeding. Schedule a referral to a hematologist for further evaluation and potential intravenous iron therapy. Arrange for a capsule endoscopy if initial investigations are inconclusive. Prescribe ferrous sulfate 25 mg twice daily with orange juice to enhance absorption. Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to reassess blood work and symptoms.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Iron deficiency anemia.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Mrs. Christina Cooper is a 65-year-old female who presents with a long-history of iron deficiency anemia.

The patient reports she has been living with anemia since she was 13 years old. She attempted to donate blood on 12/2019 but was unable to due to her anemia. She has a history of heavy menstruation and a hysterectomy in 1996. She completed a colonoscopy 5-6 years ago with nonsignificant findings. She denies having a pacemaker, defibrillator, sleep apnea, or using oxygen.

Mrs. Cooper reports a myriad of symptoms, stating she feels fatigued often during the day, feverish with chills at times, and during activity feels she cannot catch her breath and some wheezing is present. She has been having some headaches which is uncommon for her. Additionally, she is noticing tingling sensations, cold sensitivity, and her anxiety and depression have worsened.

PAST HISTORY

Surgical
Hysterectomy, 1996.

Procedures
Colonoscopy 5-6 years ago.

SOCIAL HISTORY

Drink’s alcohol 1-2 times per year.

CURRENT MEDICATIONS

Ferrous Sulfate 25 mg tablet twice per day (1 in the morning, 1 in the evening), Vitamin B12 OTC.

PHYSICAL EXAM

Gastrointestinal
Abdomen non-tender.

ASSESSMENT

• Iron deficiency anemia

Mrs. Cooper is a 65-year-old female who was diagnosed with iron deficiency anemia in 12/2019 and was unable to donate blood. Her follow-up blood work in 01/2020 revealed a low hemoglobin, stable hematocrit, and normal iron labs, although, ferritin was low. She has been taking ferrous sulfate 325 mg by mouth.

PLAN

I have asked her to continue each ferrous sulfate dose with vitamin C found in orange juice for the next 12 weeks then recheck CBC, iron ferritin, B-12, and folate. A referral was sent for her to see a hematologist. We will plan for EGD and colonoscopy to assess for potential sources of anemia or GI bleed. If this is inconclusive, capsule endoscopy can be considered.

INSTRUCTIONS

Schedule upper endoscopy, colonoscopy and appointment with hematologist. Return to the clinic if symptoms worsen.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient denies any pacemaker or defibrillator use, sleep apnea, or oxygen use at night.
section_miscategorizations.reason: These are elements of the patient's reported history and device/condition status rather than objective exam, vitals, labs, or imaging. While often documented in pre-procedure assessments, they are still history-type content rather than physical findings.
